chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Blood Money for the Nutfah (sperm), Alaqah (clinging clot), Mudghah (embryonic lump), Bone, and Fetus

text: 5324 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ اَلْفُضَيْلِ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ لِصٍّ دَخَلَ عَلَى اِمْرَأَةٍ حُبْلَى فَوَقَعَ عَلَيْهَا فَأَلْقَتْ مَا فِي بَطْنِهَا فَوَثَبَتْ عَلَيْهِ اَلْمَرْأَةُ فَقَتَلَتْهُ قَالَ "يُطَلُّ دَمُ اَللِّصِّ وَ عَلَى اَلْمَقْتُولِ دِيَةُ سَخْلَتِهَا".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5324 - Al-Husayn ibn Saʿid narrated from Muhammad ibn Al-Fuḍayl who said:
I asked Abu Al-Hasan (as) about a thief who entered upon a pregnant woman, assaulted her, and caused her to miscarry. The woman then attacked the thief and killed him.
Imam (as) replied: "The blood of the thief is not avenged (yutallu), but the diyyah (blood money) for her miscarried fetus is due upon her."
